title: Sushi Dinner
description: Compute the number of ways to choose two subsets $X, Y \subseteq \{2, 3, \dots, n\}$ such that there does not exist $x \in X, y \in Y$ such that $x$ and $y$ are not relatively prime. The sets $X, Y$ may be empty. Output the number of ways modulo $p$.
input: The input contains the integer $n$ and the modulo $p$ separated by a space.
output: Output the number of ways to choose the subsets $X, Y \subseteq \{2, 3, \dots, n\}$ satisfying the condition above.
source: Olympiad, National Olympiad in Informatics (China), NOI 2015
